last_result: Cycle 58 complete. AATE-SDT THEOREM DERIVED: CA instrument discriminability d'(n) is strictly monotonically decreasing in OA training iterations n. Derived from Signal Detection Theory (Green & Swets 1954) — fully independent of K1-K7/AIIP/TCA framework. SDT premises: P1=baseline d'(0)>0; P2=OA training weakly increases mu_N(n); P3=strict increase while gradient nonzero; P4=mu_S stable; P5=CPP ceiling. Proof: P3→mu_N(n+1)>mu_N(n)→d'(n+1)<d'(n). Asymptote: d'(inf)=mu_S(1-ceiling_AATE)/sigma>0 for CPP>0. SDT-unique predictions: Q1=A'(M_n)<A'(M_{n-1}) for successive RLHF checkpoints; Q2=cross-temporal calibration failure; Q3=d' threshold effects (d'<1.0 marginal, d'<0.5 useless); Q4=CPP moderation of d'(inf); Q5=criterion shift. EP6 CANDIDATE: A'-deterioration test across RLHF checkpoints using K5 Test 3 as CA instrument. AATE confidence: 0.76→0.78. AIIP zero-retraction cycle 6 (passive). D4 streak: 50. Document: state/decisions/aate-sdt-theorem.md.
